title: Iria sphaerocephala
intro: Iria sphaerocephala, also known as Groundsel, is an annual plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that grows in a variety of environments, including roadsides and disturbed areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, and its ability to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. Iria sphaerocephala contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into creams and salves for topical application.
